Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and microalbuminuria, indicating significant renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria) further support renal dysfunction and possible tubular injury. Low total CO₂ suggests metabolic acidosis. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria; consider hemolytic anemia or lab artifact. Mildly elevated LDL is noted. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes (e.g., diabetes, glomerulonephritis).
